titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library Exhibition Materials
Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library Exhibition Materials
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11923562.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 29, 2017 at 08:03

did.unittitle: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library exhibition materials

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library
Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signage, photographs, labels, and reproductions of collection materials created for Beinecke Library exhibitions.

did.unittitle: Signage for "Alfred Stieglitz: A selection of his papers"
Signage and labels for "Paul Rosenfeld, '12: Voyager in the arts, a memorial exhibition"
Sign for "James Purdy: His published work"
Photographs of "An Exhibition of Manuscripts, First Editions, and Photographs of Gertrude Stein"
Signage, labels, and photographs of "Sinclair Lewis, 1885-1951: A selection of manuscripts from the collection established by the author, class of 1907"
Signage and labels for "Wallace Stevens: An exhibition of his writings, 1998-1954"
Signage and labels for "H. D.: An exhibition in honor of her seventieth birthday"
Signage and labels for "Marianne Moore: An exhibit to observe her seventy-fifth birthday"
Living Portraits: Carl Van Vechten's Color Photographs of African Americans, 1939-1964

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prints of color transparency portraits by Carl Van Vechten created for the 2009 Beinecke Library exhibition, "Living Portraits." Includes copies of the exhibition catalog and labels.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two groups: framed portraits and unframed portraits.

did.unittitle: Pearl Primus, as a dancer in Hard Line Blues, 1943
Carl Van Vechten and Diahann Carroll, June 26 1955
Carl Van Vechten in Harlem on "Josephine Baker Day," May 20 1951
In the Mind's Eye: Beinecke Architecture Imagined
